entry.title: Re: Return Request Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi @erickgomez-tech

How can I have screen like application as you. Currently, I am do process automation in web provided by SAP? I am researching on Desktop Studio but can't install help.

Thank you so much.

Regards,

Tam

entry.title: SAP CAP - How to join tables without associations in CAP using java?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently working on a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) project using Java. I have encountered a scenario where I need to join two tables that are not linked with an association.

I have successfully achieved this using views, but I am exploring if it is possible to accomplish the same using CQL directly. Below is the working approach using a view:

 

   entity testView as select from schema.hu as hu left join schema.app as app on hu.huNum = app.huNum {
                      hu.desc, app.code as App_code, hu.huNum
   };

 

I came across the Join class in the com.sap.cds.impl.builder.model package, which lets you define the join type, sources, and join condition. However, I’m struggling to execute it successfully.

Additionally, I found in the CAP documentation CqnSelect, that the from clause of CqnSelect can accept a join or source. However, I am unable to pass a Join instance into the from clause. Below is the code I have attempted:

 

Select huSelect = Select.from(Hu_.class);
Select appSelect = Select.from(App_.class);
Join join = new Join(CqnJoin.Type.FULL, huSelect, appSelect,
                     CQL.comparison(CQL.get("hu.huNum"),
                                    CqnComparisonPredicate.Operator.EQ, 
                                    CQL.get("app.huNum")));

log.info("result test: {}", dao.getDb().run(Select.from(join.asSelect())));

 

Could you please guide me on:

  1. How to use the Join class to achieve this functionality.
  2. How to pass a join instance into the from clause of CqnSelect ?
  3. Any alternative approaches or best practices to handle this scenario.

Thanks in advance 😊

entry.title: Re: ABAP programming: How to zip a file with password

entry.title: Re: Confirme Dates with SAP DM
entry.content: Thanks for your reply. We are using the functionality to post the duration of different actitivies. I was just curious if we can set the start and end time as well.

entry.title: Re: Link between Subscription parameters amount for recur...
entry.content:
The price model would be the one for a single truck, but a tier:
from 0 to 200 'normalized' API calls - 0 €
beyond: 2€ / EA

Example: 2 trucks, 700 API calls -> 350 'normalized API calls'
Pricing with a tier: 200 calls -> 0 € + 150 calls * 2€ = 300 €

There's also the alternative to normalize and subtract the included quantity in a formula (calls / trucks - included calls), and use the result in a volume price model. 
Then you need to ensure that no negative quantities are calculated (can be done with the suitable quantity operator).

entry.title: Error com.tns.NativeScriptException: Attempt to use cleared object reference id=XXXXX
entry.content:
Hi,

sometime during an OData call i got in the actionresult of Failure Action the error:

com.tns.NativeScriptException: Attempt to use cleared object reference id=XXXXX

To solve that error I simply need to close and open again the app.

Seems like I got this message, in most cases, after a while of inactivity on the app.

Ex.: OData Call of Media Entity

Ex.: OData Call of Create Entity

Why this happens and what I can check ?

Can I do somethings to avoid or limitate this issue ?

Thanks.

entry.title: SalesCloud Org Structure - Org Units: In which report or datasource can we find the address fields?
entry.content:
We want to use the address fields in the org structure management. But I was not able to find a report or data source, where the address field are included. How do I get the fields "address_line_1", "address_line_2" out of the system? 

The fields I wanted to be reported are standard fields in an standard object. In the data workbench you can download them with "Organisational_Unit_Name_and_Address". But we need them in a report.

What changes did you make and for any particular reason?

entry.title: Re: New AWS option for SAP ODP data extraction through AWS Glue
entry.content:
Hi @ashuetrxsap Hi this is an excellent question that requires clarification.

SAP does not block ODP data extraction, it is a valid tool for 3rd party connectors, knowing its limitations (data must be transformed into OData and exposed in the ABAP layer, with the compute burden this takes), any tool can connect to the ODP framework for data extraction and replication through the OData API. (link to documentation) but it's forbidden to skip the ODP Odata layer and consume directly the function modules bypassing the framework (SAP Note 3255746 - Unpermitted usage of ODP Data Replication APIs)

entry.title: reverse IW8W for refurbishment GR
entry.content:
hello all

SAP ECC6: GR for refurbishment with IW8W sets status DLV to the order.

I reversed the GR by canceling the document with Migo cancellation, generating a Mvt 310.

the issue is that status DLV does not change/disappear and when I try to perform a GR again, with IW8W or MIGO, I get a message that the material has been delivered already and I cannot perform the GR again, even if the material movement in the order shows that the first GR has been reversed.

Is there a way to do that so that I can receive the material again?

entry.title: Re: CDS View: Time comparison
entry.content:
Hi Glen,

yes you are correct, its not available yet. FISCAL_CALENDER_OPERATIONs will be available in upcoming on Prem release.

Best Regards,

Lina

entry.title: Re: TRANSFERRING ECTR DOCUMENT CHARACTERISTICS TO WINDOWS
entry.content:
Hello nkara,

showing CAD file attributes within windows explorer (columns) is out of scope for ECTR. This is the responsibility of the CAD vendor and how the file format is defined.

Please check with the CAD vendor.

Hope this helps.

Gerhard

entry.title: API Testing with Tricentis Test Automation integrated with SAP Cloud ALM & SAP Test Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

APIs are the backbone of modern software architectures, enabling seamless integration and communication across systems. In this blog, we’ll explore how to perform API testing using Tricentis Test Automation for SAP integrated with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Test Automation by Tricentis—two robust, SaaS-based solutions designed to meet industry standards for reliability, scalability, and flexibility.

This article complements the previous blog, API Testing with Tricentis Test Automation for SAP (TTA) & SAP Enterprise Continuous Testing (ECT). This time, we’ll focus on the Tricentis public cloud solutions provisioned via SAP Cloud ALM, as shown in pink in the illustration below.

 

How to Test an API with Tricentis Public Cloud Testing Solution?

1. Understand the API through its documentation

  • Familiarize yourself with the API’s endpoints, request/response payloads, authentication methods, and any relevant information about the API's behavior and functionality.
  • Use this information to plan your test cases and strategy.

2. Create and Execute an API Test in Tosca Cloud

  1. Open your Tosca Cloud tenant and navigate to the “API Playground.”
  2. Define the name for your API call
  3. Enter the API endpoint URL, headers, and authentication details, then click "Send" to trigger the request.
  4. Review the response displayed to verify the API behavior.

3. Define Verification Parameters

  • Add validation criteria such as:
    • Status Code Validation: Ensure the response returns the expected status code.
    • Payload Validation: Check specific values within the response payload
  • Use the "Verification" tab to set these parameters and re-execute the request to validate.
  • Click on "Create API test case"

4. Use the API Action in your Test Case

  • You can directly execute the API test case or incorporate it into a larger test scenario.
  • Drag and drop the API action into your test script and execute seamlessly by clicking on "Run".

What’s Next: Service Simulation with SAP Test Automation by Tricentis

In conclusion, API testing plays a vital role in ensuring software quality. With Tricentis Test Automation integrated into SAP Cloud ALM, testers gain the ability to streamline API test creation and execution within a fully cloud-based framework.

Looking ahead, our next blog will delve into API Service Simulation using SAP Test Automation by Tricentis, showcasing how virtual services can replicate real-world APIs for more sophisticated testing scenarios.

Stay connected by following my profile for updates, and don’t hesitate to share your questions or feedback in the comments below.

Wishing you joyful holiday celebrations and a wonderful end to the year!

Thank you for reading!

entry.title: Re: Custom Tab
entry.content: We do not have any UI configuration templates set up that I can see

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Integration with Board
entry.content:
Hi there,

has anyone yet tried to integrate Datasphere data with Board? We have tried to connect the two insatnces via API's but this unfortunately did not work. If connection does not work an automated way to import/export data between the two instances would also be very helpful.

Many thanks in advance,

Moritz

entry.title: Re: Warn users before logging them off
entry.content:
Hello,

I am not familiar with ABAP channels, on a first glance it looks maybe a little bit too complicated and too oversized for this "simple" issue. 

I am more with @raymond_giuseppi and the proposal of using TH_POPUP.

... Later I realized that I can't show a popup to the user from a background job.

It should be possible - although you are going to run the program calling TH_POPUP in the background, the popup should appear at the frontend of the targeted users. Did you try it?

Kind regards

Jan

entry.title: Re: Delta processing inside SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
One Question why do you use as Change Type L instead of I for new records? In the help, it is documented as I 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/c8a54ee704e94e15926551293243fd1d/154bdffb35814d5481d1f6de143a6b9e.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: TRANSFERRING ECTR DOCUMENT CHARACTERISTICS TO WINDOWS
entry.content: Hi Gerhard, The ones I show in the picture are not CAD parameters, they are characteristics defined in SAP and matched with ECTR. They are generally needed, specially defined characteristics. My opinion is that if ECTR included this subject in its scope, it would gain a very different dimension. If it is still not possible, there is nothing to do. 🙂 Thanks

entry.title: Re: App Manage Bank Accounts (F1366A) – Error in create p...

entry.title: Re: Performance Issues in SAP Analytics Cloud with SAP BW/4HANA live connection
entry.content: Are you creating classic story or Optimized design experience story? Have you looked the detailed logs of SAC story execution to see where it is taking the most of time? If you are using optimized design mode, have you looked at the performance insights available when running with performance Analysis ?

entry.title: Creating Custom Client IDs in SAP Document Information Extraction Trial Version
entry.content:
I'm currently working on a requirement using the trial version of the SAP Document Information Extraction service through Postman. While extracting data, I noticed that I'm only getting the default client ID, but I need to create a custom client ID for my custom documents. Is there any way to achieve this with the trial version of SAP, or is there another method I can use?

To provide some context, I have set up my SAP BTP trial account and subscribed to the Document Information Extraction service. I navigated to the SAP BTP Cockpit, created a new service instance, and generated the service keys, which included the client ID and client secret required for authentication. I configured custom documents by specifying the document types and extraction rules in my service instance. Using the generated client ID and client secret in my Postman requests, I was able to authenticate and extract data from my custom documents. However, I'm still limited to the default client ID.

If anyone has experience with this or knows if it's possible to create a custom client ID with the trial version, or if there's another way to achieve this, I would greatly appreciate your insights. Additionally, if the trial version does not support this feature, would upgrading to a paid version provide the necessary flexibility and additional features to manage custom client IDs more effectively?

entry.title: Re: Performance Issues in SAP Analytics Cloud with SAP BW/4HANA live connection
entry.content:
Hello @ChinkyGarg 

we are using Optimized design and yes we have seen that most of time is consumed on loading data on the widgets created.

entry.title: Run Executable JAR Files on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Run Executable JAR Files on SAP BTP

Introduction

I received a requirement to develop an interface for payment files with a bank as part of an S/4HANA implementation project. This project involved migrating from the existing SAP ECC6 on-premise + SAP PI/PO setup to S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition + SAP BTP-CPI.

The payment files needed to be encrypted or hashed using the bank’s proprietary executable JAR files before being sent. Here's a brief overview of the requirements:

  1. Bank A: Encrypt files using unknow algorithm (likely AES) with the following command:
    java -jar Bank-A-Encrypt.jar <input-file-full-path> <output-file-full-path>
     
  2. Bank B: Hash files using unknow algorithm (not MD5) with the following command:
    java -Dcustom_config.properties="./config/custom_config.properties" -jar Bank-B-Hash.jar <input-file-full-path> <output-file-full-path>
     

In the previous setup, SAP PI/PO handled this task by running OS commands on the communication channel. However, with the new cloud-based solution, I couldn’t use standard Java libraries and had to rely on the bank's provided executable JAR files.

Challenges

Running executable JAR files in a cloud-based solution like S/4HANA Cloud + SAP CPI posed several challenges. Here are the solutions I tried and why they didn't work:

Solution 1: Run on S/4HANA with AL11 Shared Drive

  • Problem:
    • S/4HANA Cloud does not allow installing a JRE.
    • OS commands like java -jar ... cannot be executed on the AL11 shared drive.

Solution 2: Run on the User's Desktop

  • Problem:
    • SAP GUI is not allowed on user desktops.
    • OS commands cannot be executed via Web GUI.

Solution 3: Run via SAP CPI

  • Problem:
    • Executable JAR files cannot be run directly in SAP CPI iFlows.

After trying these approaches, I found a workaround that worked in my case.

Solution: Wrap and Run the Executable JAR in a Custom Java Application on SAP BTP

I developed a Java program to wrap and run the executable JAR files, then deployed it on SAP BTP. To simplify the implementation, I used Spring Boot.

Here’s an overview of the architecture and development steps:

  1. Create a Spring Boot application to wrap the executable JAR files provided by the bank. Use ProcessBuilder to execute the JAR files.
  2. Read and write intermediate files by encoding and decoding them as base64 strings. These files are processed using the application container’s ephemeral storage.
  3. Expose the application as a REST API to accept and return base64-encoded payment files.
  4. Deploy the application on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry as a Java application.
  5. Secure the API using SAP XSUAA services.
  6. Integrate with SAP S/4HANA via CPI iFlows.
  7. Develop an ABAP program to handle base64 (xstring) files on AL11.

In this blog, I'll focus on Steps 1-4 and demonstrate the solution using a mock JAR file to simulate the scenario.

Implementation Details

Step 0: Simulate the Bank’s Executable JAR

To simulate the bank’s JAR file, I created a mock JAR file. Running the following command processes an input file and generates an encrypted output file in the same folder:

java -jar bank-executable-mock.jar <input-file-full-path>
 

Example Code (BankExecutableMock.java)

package com.binlah.sap.btp.appdev;

import java.io.*;
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;

public class BankExecutableMock {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        if (args.length < 1) {
            System.err.println("Usage: java BankExecutableMock <inputFile>");
            return;
        }

        String inputFilePath = args[0];
        String outputFilePath = getOutputFileName(inputFilePath);

        // Read content from the input file
        String fileContent = readFromFile(inputFilePath);
        if (fileContent == null) {
            System.err.println("Failed to read from the input file.");
            return;
        }

        // Process the content
        String processedContent = "Encrypted((" + fileContent.trim() + "))";

        // Write processed content to the output file
        writeToFile(outputFilePath, processedContent);
    }

    public static String getOutputFileName(String inputFileName) {
        // Replace the extension with ".txt"
        return inputFileName.replaceAll("\\.\\w+$", ".encrypt");
    }

    public static void writeToFile(String filePath, String content) {
        try (BufferedWriter writer = new BufferedWriter(
                new OutputStreamWriter(new FileOutputStream(filePath), StandardCharsets.UTF_8))) {
            writer.write(content);
            System.out.println("Content written to file successfully: " + filePath);
        } catch (IOException e) {
            System.err.println("Error writing to file: " + e.getMessage());
        }
    }

    public static String readFromFile(String filePath) {
        StringBuilder content = new StringBuilder();
        try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
                new InputStreamReader(new FileInputStream(filePath), StandardCharsets.UTF_8))) {
            String line;
            while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
                content.append(line).append(System.lineSeparator());
            }
        } catch (IOException e) {
            System.err.println("Error reading from file: " + e.getMessage());
            return null;
        }
        return content.toString();
    }
}
 

Example Input File (input.txt)

This is my payment file.
 

Example Result File (input.encrypt)

Encrypted((This is my payment file.))
 

Step 1: Develop the Wrapper Application

Use Spring Initializr to create a Spring Boot application. Inside the application, use ProcessBuilder to run the executable JAR.

Create a Spring Boot Application

Generate a Spring Boot project with the following dependencies:

  • Spring Web
  • Spring Security
  • OAuth2 Resource Server
  • Spring Boot Actuator (optional, for monitoring)

After creating the Spring project, extract the files and copy bank-executable-mock.jar into the src/resources folder. Initially, comment out security-related dependencies in pom.xml to skip authentication during testing.

Example Code (pom.xml):

<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
    </dependency>
    <!-- Security dependencies (commented for now) -->
    <!-- Uncomment these after testing -->
</dependencies>
 

Create a new controller class to receive HTTP requests as shown below.

Example Code (EncryptionController.java)

package com.binlah.sap.btp.appdev.bank_wrapper;

import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.*;
import org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity;
import java.io.FileNotFoundException;
import java.io.InputStream;
import java.nio.file.*;
import java.util.*;
import java.io.OutputStreamWriter;
import java.io.BufferedWriter;
import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;

@RestController
@RequestMapping("/api")
public class EncryptionController {

    @PostMapping("/encrypt")
    public ResponseEntity<Map<String, String>> encryptFile(
            @RequestBody Map<String, String> request) {
        try {
            String rootPath = "C:/Dev/Bank";
            String javaPath = "java";

            // Extract input fields
            String inputFileName = request.get("input_file");
            String base64Input = request.get("input_data");

            if (inputFileName == null || inputFileName.isEmpty()) {
                return ResponseEntity.badRequest()
                        .body(Map.of("error", "Input field 'input_file' is missing or empty."));
            }

            if (base64Input == null || base64Input.isEmpty()) {
                return ResponseEntity.badRequest()
                        .body(Map.of("error", "Input field 'input_data' is missing or empty."));
            }

            // Generate unique folder for the request
            String uniqueFolderName = UUID.randomUUID().toString();
            Path uniqueFolderPath = Paths.get(rootPath, uniqueFolderName);
            Files.createDirectories(uniqueFolderPath);

            // Decode Base64 input
            byte[] decodedBytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(base64Input);

            // Write input file with UTF-8
            Path inputFilePath = uniqueFolderPath.resolve(inputFileName);
            writeUtf8(inputFilePath, decodedBytes);

            // Define output file path with dynamic extension
            String outputFileName = getOutputFileName(inputFileName);
            Path outputFilePath = uniqueFolderPath.resolve(outputFileName);

            // Run the external JAR to process the file
            ProcessBuilder processBuilder = new ProcessBuilder(javaPath, "-jar", getJarPath(),
                    inputFilePath.toString());

            Process process = processBuilder.start();
            int exitCode = process.waitFor();
            if (exitCode != 0) {
                return ResponseEntity.status(500)
                        .body(Map.of("error", "Encryption process failed."));
            }

            // Read the output file and encode it to Base64
            byte[] encryptedFileBytes = Files.readAllBytes(outputFilePath);
            String base64Encrypted = Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(encryptedFileBytes);

            // Clean up the folder
            // cleanUpDirectory(uniqueFolderPath);

            // Return the response in the specified format
            return ResponseEntity
                    .ok(Map.of("output_file", outputFileName, "output_data", base64Encrypted));

        } catch (Exception e) {
            e.printStackTrace();
            return ResponseEntity.status(500).body(Map.of("error", e.getMessage()));
        }
    }

    private String getOutputFileName(String inputFileName) {
        // Replace the extension with ".txt"
        return inputFileName.replaceAll("\\.\\w+$", ".encrypt");
    }

    private String getJarPath() throws Exception {
        // Extract the bundled JAR to a temporary location
        InputStream jarStream = getClass().getResourceAsStream("/bank-executable-mock.jar");
        if (jarStream == null) {
            throw new FileNotFoundException("Bundled JAR not found.");
        }
        Path tempJarPath = Files.createTempFile("bank-executable-mock", ".jar");
        Files.copy(jarStream, tempJarPath, StandardCopyOption.REPLACE_EXISTING);
        tempJarPath.toFile().deleteOnExit();
        return tempJarPath.toString();
    }

    private void writeUtf8(Path filePath, byte[] content) throws Exception {
        // Write UTF-8
        try (BufferedWriter writer = new BufferedWriter(
                new OutputStreamWriter(Files.newOutputStream(filePath), StandardCharsets.UTF_8))) {
            // Write content
            writer.write(new String(content, StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
        }
    }

    private void cleanUpDirectory(Path directoryPath) throws Exception {
        // Recursively delete files and the directory
        Files.walk(directoryPath).sorted(Comparator.reverseOrder()).forEach(path -> {
            try {
                Files.delete(path);
            } catch (Exception e) {
                e.printStackTrace();
            }
        });
    }
}
 

Step 2: Run Locally

Encode files as base64 strings to process them, then run the Spring Boot application on your local machine and test it using Curl/Postman.

URL

http://localhost:8080/api/encrypt
 

Request

{
  "input_file": "input.txt",
  "input_data": "VGhpcyBpcyBteSBwYXltZW50IGZpbGUu"
}
 

Response

{
  "output_file": "input.encrypt",
  "output_data": "RW5jcnlwdGVkKChUaGlzIGlzIG15IHBheW1lbnQgZmlsZS4pKQ"
}
 

You can also verify the results by checking the temporary files in the generated folder.

Step 3: Handle File Storage and Correct Java Runtime Path

Before deploying to BTP Cloud Foundry, update the code as follows:

  1. Change the Java runtime to the full path within the Cloud Foundry environment.
  2. Switch file storage from the local machine to ephemeral storage within the application container.
  3. Optionally, uncomment the method cleanUpDirectory() to clean up temporary files after processing.

Example Code (EncryptionController.java)

// Modify file paths and Java runtime as shown below

// Old paths
// String rootPath = "C:/Dev/Bank";
// String javaPath = "java";

// Updated paths for SAP BTP
String rootPath = "/tmp";
String javaPath = "/home/vcap/app/META-INF/.sap_java_buildpack/sap_machine_jdk/bin/java";

// Uncomment to clean up the folder after processing
cleanUpDirectory(uniqueFolderPath);
 

Step 4: Deploy on SAP BTP

Modify the application to support deployment on SAP BTP, and create a manifest.yml file.

Example Code (manifest.yml)

applications:
  - name: bank-wrapper
    path: ./target/bank-wrapper-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
    random-route: true
    memory: 1024M
    buildpacks:
      - sap_java_buildpack_jakarta
    env:
      TARGET_RUNTIME: tomcat
      JBP_CONFIG_COMPONENTS: "jres: ['com.sap.xs.java.buildpack.jdk.SAPMachineJDK']"
      JBP_CONFIG_SAP_MACHINE_JDK: '{ version: 21.+ }'
 

Deployment Commands

mvn clean install

cf api https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com

cf login --sso

cf push
 

Test the API after deployment to verify it behaves as expected.

If the Java path is not updated, the following error may occur:

{
  "error": "Cannot run program "java": error=2, No such file or directory"
}
 

Step 5: Secure the Application (Optional)

Enable security capabilities by integrating XSUAA.

Example Code (xs-security.json)

{
  "xsappname": "bank-wrapper",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "scopes": [
	  {
		  "name": "$XSAPPNAME.api_access",
		  "description": "Access to the API"
	  }
  ],
  "role-templates": [
	  {
		  "name": "API_ACCESS",
		  "description": "Role for API access",
		  "scope-references": [
			  "$XSAPPNAME.api_access"
		  ]
	  }
  ],
  "oauth2-configuration": {
	  "redirect-uris": [
		  "https://*.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/**"
	  ]
  }
}
 

Example Code (SecurityConfig.java)

package com.binlah.sap.btp.appdev.bank_wrapper;

import org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;
import org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;
import org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity;
import org.springframework.security.oauth2.jwt.JwtDecoder;
import org.springframework.security.oauth2.jwt.NimbusJwtDecoder;
import org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain;

@Configuration
public class SecurityConfig {

    @Bean
    public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
        http.authorizeHttpRequests(
                auth -> auth.requestMatchers("/api/*").authenticated().anyRequest().permitAll())
                .oauth2ResourceServer(oauth2 -> oauth2.jwt(jwt -> jwt.decoder(jwtDecoder())));
        return http.build();
    }

    @Bean
    public JwtDecoder jwtDecoder() {
        String jwkSetUri = "https://[your sub account].authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/token_keys";
        return NimbusJwtDecoder.withJwkSetUri(jwkSetUri).build();
    }
}
 

Update manifest.yml to include the XSUAA service:

  applications:
  - name: bank-wrapper
    path: ./target/bank-wrapper-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
    random-route: true
    memory: 1024M
    buildpacks:
      - sap_java_buildpack_jakarta
    env:
      TARGET_RUNTIME: tomcat
      JBP_CONFIG_COMPONENTS: "jres: ['com.sap.xs.java.buildpack.jdk.SAPMachineJDK']"
      JBP_CONFIG_SAP_MACHINE_JDK: '{ version: 21.+ }'
    services:
      - bank-xsuaa-instance
 

Deployment Command:

mvn clean install

cf create-service xsuaa application bank-xsuaa-instance -c xs-security.json

cf create-service-key bank-xsuaa-instance defaultKey

# (optional) check service key
cf service-key bank-xsuaa-instance defaultKey

# Deploy application
cf push

cf bind-service bank-wrapper bank-xsuaa-instance

cf restage bank-wrapper
 

Now, the API is secured with OAuth 2.0 by using Bearer {{token}}.

https://[your sub account].authentication.us10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
 

Conclusion

This approach allowed me to handle executable JAR files in a cloud-based environment on SAP BTP while addressing challenges like file storage and security. I hope this guide helps other developers facing similar requirements.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order - Level Approval Workflow and release from Credit Block 2 in 1
entry.content: ...found it 🙂 have declare it in private section of Class zso_approval_exit...

entry.title: Upgrading to CAP CDS 8: A living document of fixes and lessons learned
entry.content:
During our upgrade to CAP CDS 8, my colleague @vvdries and me encountered a number of 'challenges' to be tackled. In this document, we aim to share our findings with you.

This living document not only highlights what we’ve learned but also invites the community to contribute their own bugs and fixes. Our goal is to update this post regularly with new insights, additional bugs, or improved solutions. So, feel free to share your experiences and fixes in the comments.

A general advise is to plan this upgrade. According to the size of your project lot of testing is needed, and fixing all issues might take up a lot of time.

Finding 1: Entities Must Have a Key

If you did not provide keys, just simply add them and do NOT suppress the error message with a warning as this will only cause other issues.

If you have calculated associations, where there is no key, you can use types. (Be aware that according to the oData spec each entity should have a key)

 

 

@cds.persistense.skip 
entity NonpersistentEntity { //Filled in handler 
   propA: Decimal; 
   propB: String; 
} 

type NonPersistent: Association to NonpersistentEntity; 

entity persistentEntity: cuid { 
   propC:Boolean; 
   propD:Date; 
   oneCalculatedEntity: NonPersistent; 
   manyCalculatedEntities: array of NonPersistent; 
}

 

 

Finding 2: Large queries fail

The issue caused by the system exceeding the default maximum packet size, was fixable by applying the instruction from this note: MAX_PACKET_SIZE = Math.pow(2,18). The hdb module is now included in the @sap-js/hana module (Using SAP HANA Cloud for Production | capire) as default driver, where these parameters are read from a different place, the package.json file.

So to solve this you need to set the parameters in the package.json file:

 

 

"cds": { 
  "requires": { 
    "[production]": { 
      "db": { 
        "kind": "hana", 
        "credentials": { 
        "packetSize": 1073741823, 
        "packetSizeLimit": 1073741823 
      } 
   } 
} 
}

 

 

Finding 3: Omitting/time-traveling temporal on CDS model/service level does not work anymore

Before CDS 8 it was possible to retrieve all entries from a table with temporal aspect, meaning also those that are not valid anymore according to the current date. This was possible using these annotations, as stated in these release notes. The where-clause for the validity date was omitted in the generated Hana Cloud DB view. 

 

 

//Entity with temporal aspect (validFrom/validTo)
entity temporalEntity : cuid, temporal {
    value : Decimal;
}

//Omit temporal aspect
define view allEntities as
    select from temporalEntity {
        key ID,
            @(cds.valid.from: false)
        key validFrom,
            @(cds.valid.to: false)
            validTo,
            value
    }

 

 

When switching to CDS 8 the @SAP/cds-compiler node also needed to be upgraded, and we noticed only 'valid' entries were retrievable from the database, meaning selecting an entry where the current date is not between  validFrom and validTo was not possible anymore. To activate this again you need to activate this (now beta...) feature in the package.json:

Please note retrieving 'invalid' entries is also possible in a handler by selecting directly from the database instead of the service or using odata url parameters  This finding only concerns CDL.

 

 

"cds": {
    "cdsc": {
      "beta": {
        "temporalRawProjection": true
      }
    }
}

 

 

Finding 4: A deep create (oData) produced error: Property "x" does not exist in XXXItemSet

The new Protocol Adapters (GA) causes logic errors when sending a create payload containing an association. They should only be creatable when it is a composition. You can skip this by adding the feature below, but don’t!

 

 

cds.features.odata_new_adapter = false

 

As CAP docu mentions in new cds upgrades this will not be available anymore.

To fix this we used the annotation @CDS.validate: false on the association. Although this fixes this issue, it does not seem like a good or permanent solution. This issue was also raised in this post
Ideally an associated entity is not part of the payload when creating a new entry, but this is not always possible.

 

entity Parent : cuid {
    propA                              : Decimal;
    propB                              : String;
    @cds.validate: false 
    childEntities : Association to Child;
}

entry.title: Re: Schedule Post Closing and Opening Balances - Italy
entry.content: Hi Lucy,

entry.title: Re: Schedule Post Closing and Opening Balances - Italy

I have one question about this point: "Normally we suggest customer conduct the year-end closing for all ledgers since the beginning balance should be accurate for reporting." - what do you mean by this? Balance carryforward itself is schedule automatically 2 weeks before YEC. Does "Schedule Post Closing and Opening Balances - Italy" has any impact on it? During testing I did not notice anything. Thanks,

entry.title: Re: XML Namespace Error with XPATH condition
entry.content: Hi Barath, You can use this xml routing condition:- //*:innererror/*:errordetails/*:errordetail[contains(*:message, 'Document')]

entry.title: Re: I am getting the error : 'Artifact upload failed due to an unexpected error.'
entry.content: Hello, I have the same error, did you find any solution? Grateful

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content: Yea

entry.title: Re: ASA 17.0.10: Question about calling external web services and passing binary data to them
entry.content:
Are you sure HTTP allows for multiple "Content-Disposition" headers? AFAIK multiple headers with the same name are primarily allowed for "Set-Cookie" headers. - I don't know whether you can add the values comma-separated in one single header line.

In my understanding of the docs, listing a header field name within the HEADER clause does modify the previous field with the same name, so the second "Content-Disposition" entry overwrites the first, so the behaviour you are noticing seems expected.

entry.title: Re: Calculate share in SAC on total amount for article
entry.content: Thanks! Works perfectly!

entry.title: Recording and slide deck of SAP on IBM i December webcast available for download
entry.content:
In our SAP on IBM i Webcast "New IBM i Migrate While Active feature and SAP system Resiliency using IBM Power" on December 11th, 2024, IBM Senior Technical Staff Member Kris Whitney provided an introduction to resiliency topics and explained how IBM i and Power technology can avoid outages or quickly recover from them. In the second part of the presentation, he introduced the new product IBM i Migrate While Active, which enables easy migrations of IBM i applications from on-premises installations into PowerVS at minimum application downtime.

As usual, the recording and slide deck of the webcast have been made available for download at the link http://ibm.biz/RadioSAPIBMi. You can find it there in the folder 2024, subfolder December 2024. If you missed the webcast or want to look at some aspects in more detail, you can look at the provided material and watch the recording at your own speed.

The link is accessible by invitation only. If you are not yet a user of Box, it will be necessary to register (free of charge) at Box with the company email address that will be used for the download. If you have previously downloaded recordings or slide decks of SAP on IBM i webcasts from this link, you should already be authorized to it. If this is the first time you are trying to download material from this link, or if your access was lost for whatever reason, you can request access by sending an email to ibmsapevents@ibm.com and providing your name, company name, country, requested company email address (if different from sender), as well as the requested webcast date and title.

entry.title: Re: validate an input field in SAP Build Apps.
entry.content:
Use formula like this:

 

IF(MATCHES_REGEX("1234567890123a", "^[0-9]{12}$"),"MATCH","BOOOOOOOOOOOO!")

 

 Obviously, replaces the "123..." string with your variable.

P.S.: Thanks, ChatGPT 😺

entry.title: Re: SAC Add dimensions as properties
entry.content:
You can convert a dimension into property and attach it another dimension: 

For disable planning capability, if you hover on the disabled button, you may find info why this switch is disabled. One example as below:

entry.title: Number of occurrences SAP BW SAC
entry.content:
Hello!

I wonder if someone could help me with a problem I have.

I have a DSO with key article and day and it contains the days the article has been in backorder.

I have calculated the number of days the article has been in backorder, so far no problems.

But now I want to have the occasions it has been in backorder for a given timespan.

In the graph below the selected article has two occasions with backorder Nov 10 - Nov 24 and Dec 11- Dec 12.  So next to the backorder days 15, I want the number of occasions 2.

Does anyone know how I can accomplish this?

entry.title: Triger workflow for time off requests
entry.content:
Hello All.

Maybe someone can help with the next task. 

We have a time type (LOA) - for example, MatLeave.

It needs to configure a workflow with next condition: 

When a user creates this leave for the first time the workflow must inform a specific role (HR). If the user changes Start date or Expected Return Date for this leave (saved before) the workflow must not be triggered. If the user adds to the leave Actual Return Date the workflow must inform a specific role again. 

So, the specific role must be  informed if  the leave is created for the first time and when the leave updates Expected Return Date. 

 

Standart case with workflow on time type in the field Workflow Configuration triggers workflow for any changes. 

I think about object definition of Employee Time. I mean Save Rules. But I cannot find a solution to my case. 

 

Did somebody come across with same requirements? Any recommendation, please. 

Thank you. 

SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking HCM Time Management SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: Re: Need validation for PO print PDF to be non-editable after downloading it from SAP
entry.content: Did you solve it?

entry.title: unable to use targetElement within ui.facet when extending a standard cds view
entry.content:
Hi, 
I tried to extend the ui.facet of a standard cds view with a new metadata extension. The purpose is to extend the object page of a fiori element app with a new section and present it as table (type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE). First I extended the standard app with a new association to my ZCDSView which it has also a metadata extension file with annotation (@UI.LINEITEM).
I did also a metadata extension to the standard app on the customer layer and add a new label to the UI.facet as follow:
@ui.facet: [

{

id: 'addr',
label: 'address',
pupose: #standard,
type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
position: 230,
targetElement: '_ZCDSView'                //defined as an alias of the association in the extension of the standard cds

}]

The problem is that The section 'address' is not appearing at all in the object page. But when removing the targetElement: '_ZCDSView',  the label appear with empty content.

I am also sure that the association has been defined properly in the extended view because the association can be followed in the data preview mode.
Any solution to this problem?

entry.title: Situation Handling in Handling Unit Management In S/4 Hana
entry.content:
Hi Readers

Today I am here with new features in Handling Unit Management which is called situation handling. Here I will explain only conceptual knowledge for it. Please go through it and advise me to improve if it is required.

Brief Introduction to Handling Unit and its features

Handling Unit

A handling unit is a physical unit consisting of packaging materials and the goods contained on it. A handling unit is always a combination of materials and packaging materials

 

Nested Handling Unit

Handling units can be nested, and we can create a new handling unit from several other handling units.

 

Note- A handling unit has a unique, scannable identification number that we can construct according to industry standards such as GSI-128 or SSCC. For Example, Label Printing for EAN128 and Adapting the Label Layout.

We can use Handling Unit Management to map packing-controlled logistics in the SAP system. Here, we do not consider the movements of the individual materials, instead we map the movements of the handling unit  that contain these materials. Using handling units to map logistical processes simplifies processing of goods movements and thus optimizes logistics processes.

This simplified process includes the existing packing function in shipping and the storage unit processing in the Warehouse Management system, which have been enhanced here.

Some Handling Unit Features

  • A handling unit has a unique, scannable identification number that we can construct according to industry standards such as GSI-128 or SSCC.
  • The movability of the handling unit can be restricted if the quality inspection is active. Changes to the stock category as a result of a quality inspection are carried out through a transfer posting of the handling unit.
  • Reporting is available with which handling units can be found and displayed according to various selection criteria, such as material, packing instruction, or storage location.
  • Handling Unit Situation

Handling Unit Integration

Handling units can be used in all processes within the supply chain.

  • Sales and Distribution
  • Production
  • Purchasing
  • Goods receipt
  • Quality Management
  • Warehouse Management
  • Inventory Management
  • Shipping

 

Processes are supported

  • Goods Movements with Handling Units
  • Packing for Inbound and Outbound Deliveries
  • Handling Units in Production
  • Handling Units in Warehouse
  • Handling Units in Inventory Management
  • Physical Inventory management

Situation Handling Concept in Handling Unit in S/4 Hana

The consuming app is a business app for which this template is designed, and which displays a situation message. For some products shipped with handling units it is critical, that during the delivery certain basic conditions are met, for example, regarding the temperature, the humidity, or vibrations. To monitor these handling units, we can attach sensors to the handling unit before it leaves our warehouse to make sure that we get informed when defined thresholds are exceeded. The sensors send their data to a third party Internet of Things (IoT) system, which then creates a situation using the Handling Unit API to inform the shipping specialist. The person receives this notification, which leads to the relevant entry in the Monitor Handling Units (SAP S/4 Hana Cloud) or My situation (S/4 Hana on Premises) app. In the app, the shipping specialist can see, for example, which handling unit is affected and what the sensor detected. We can adapt the displayed texts to our own business purposes.

 Important Instruction-If a situation has been created for a specific handling unit and the situation has been resolved by the shipping specialist, no further situation can be triggered for the same handling unit. In the situation message displayed in the app, please consider the following:

  • The external handing unit number is displayed with leading zeros. If we copy the number, make sure to omit these leading zeros
  • The unit of measure is displayed as internal SAP code or ISO code only.

Role of Handling Unit IOT

Template used for Situation Handling that informs users about what is happening with a handling unit. The information is based on SAP Internet of Things sensor values, for example, an exceeded temperature or humidity value for a product packed in the handling unit that must be handled with care. We want your shipping specialists to be informed automatically when somethings happen to a handling unit.

How Handling Unit IOT works

 In SAP Internet of Things, we can configure sensors. Before a handling unit leaves your warehouse, we can attach these sensors to monitor your handling units. The sensors can,

For example, measure the temperature, the humidity, the force that is exerted on the unit, or vibrations. These values usually indicate that the goods could be damaged (because, for example, the temperature or humidity is too high, or the package was dropped).

Whenever these measured values exceed the threshold that we define in the rules, this information is sent back to SAP S/4HANA. In SAP S/4HANA, we can set up conditions to trigger a notification in the system. 

The shipping specialist receives this notification, which leads to the relevant entry in the Monitor Handling Units /My Situation app. In the app, the shipping specialist can see, for example, which handling unit is affected and what the sensor detected. You can adapt the displayed texts to your own business purposes. Since the shipping specialist receives this information as soon as the defined situation occurs, it is possible to react fast and immediately send a substitute product to the customer while the original delivery is still on the way. This saves time, reduces the risk of customer dissatisfaction while boosting your reputation as a reliable supplier.

Situation Display

 When situations occur, users are informed about them by various texts, such as in-app situation messages or notifications on SAP Fiori launchpad. In this section, we can see all preconfigured texts.

Conclusion - Situation handling is an alert for handling unit which can be sent to concerned team to spoil the product. It benefits to business and reduce the damages.

Reference https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/25a41481f62e469ba0e61015a0d39d20/4134bb536b13b44ce10000000a174cb4.html

entry.title: how to add header text in fm SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE
entry.content:
I am testing in FM "SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE". I am passing "APPLOBJECT" as "VBBK", "TEXT_ID" as "0001", "LINEAMOUNT" as "00001" in table "TEXTHEADERS_EX" and "APPLOBJECT" as "VBBK", "TEXT_ID" as "0001", "LINEAMOUNT" as "00001" and in "LINE" I pass the header text in table "TEXTLINES_EX" . But only sales order gets created. When I open the order in VA03 header text does not get updated. Note I do testing with following test sequence:

SD_SALESDOCUMENT_CREATE
BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT

Please tell if I am testing in wrong direction.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Disable global filter on charts
entry.content:
I would suggest not to use story(global) month filter, instead use page filter(input control) with range filter referring to the same current date picker( at story level). 

Below is an simple example with acquired model, I assume you can do similar with your live HANA model. The above bar chart is filtered on only one month with the page filter on the right, while the lower bar chart is filtered on past 24 months with the page filter beside it. Both page filters are using the current date picker on top.  You may also hide both page filter as user only need to pick a month in the current date picker.

entry.title: Re: SFTP Integrations and APIs error
entry.content: You setup an anonymous SFTP server on your local machine that is registered in the cloud connector as virtualtest.com?

i checked SU53  ,/IWFND/ERROR_LOG and MDCLOG there is no error.

But in SLG1 there is just a this error.

Do you have recommendation?

Thank you for support.

SAP Master Data Governance  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Problem with Content-Length header element in HTTP Request
entry.content: DATA(one_part) = request->add_multipart( suppress_content_length = abap_true ).

entry.title: Re: CL_HTTP_CLIENT, Content-Length in multipart segment are not part of RFC 186
entry.content: DATA(one_part) = request->add_multipart( suppress_content_length = abap_true ).

entry.title: Insert text type and content in notes tab in Edit Freight Unit Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi all, I have a single Freight order number and some stop Ids. I have two requirements:-

1). I first need to find all the Freight unit number for each stop ID.

2). I need to insert the text type and content in the notes tab of the Edit Freight Unit Fiori App for each of the Freight unit number fetched above. In other words, I need to insert text content in text collection node for the BO /SCMTMS/root after expanding the nodes through tor_id.

I need ABAP code for both these requirements. Please let me know if any more info is required. If anyone can help even on one of the requirements, please help as it is crucial and urgent for me. Thank you in advance.

@SandyTran @Sandra_Rossi @OliverGraeff @jutta_weissing-hess @ChenNee @camilapetry @Marco_Valencia @WouterLemaire@Marian_Zeis @MRobalinho@Ryan-Crosby@RaghavanKathamuthu

Apologies to all tagged people in advance since I don't get any replies when I don't tag anyone and this is very crucial and urgent for me.

entry.title: Field addition in Screen Maintenance Requests app
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I need to add an Equipment Sort Field to Screen Maintenance App.

For this in which view I need to add the field.

Kindly let me know the procedure for to add a field to the app or in which BADI we can add logic.

Thanks,

Sai Bhargav.

entry.title: Re: Delete Primary Values from Numeric Point Chart
entry.content:
Hi,
try this:
change 'Chart_2' and 'ID OF YOUR MEASURE' accordingly.

if you want to add multiple measures you can do it one after another like in row 7 or you use the same technique from removing the measures.

var measure = Chart_2.getMeasures(Feed.ValueAxis);

for (var i = 0; i < measure.length; i++) {
	Chart_2.removeMeasure(measure[i], Feed.ValueAxis);
}

Chart_2.addMeasure("ID OF YOUR MEASURE", Feed.ValueAxis);

br

edgar

entry.title: Re: How to Customize scan QR Code in WM?
entry.content: Thank you Dominik

entry.title: Re: RAP : To Show Child or not to Show Child, depending o...
entry.content: okay, I made a virtual element and in the method IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ~CALCULATE I determined the value of the boolean field 'hiddenpgm'. The virtual element I declared as follows: @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZR_CL_MPDF_PROGRAMMA' virtual hiddenpgm: abap_boolean,

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Language of Folders
entry.content:
I wish I had an answer for you instead of a recommendation: contact Signavio support. It could be a bug. They have been really helpful on our learning journey these last few months.

We use English (workspace default) and French. I was able to get the translation to behave as expected (based on your scenario) in the Process Collaboration Hub, but not the Process Manager. I wonder if this is because our workspace default is English, so everything needs to be developed in English first. 

I'd be interested to learn what you find out, if you don't mind sharing once you've reached a conclusion.

Good luck!

Shawn

entry.title: SAPUI5: Wrong Deviation Calculation for KPI/DataPoint
entry.content:
I have an SAPUI5 application that is based on an FIORI Elements Overview Page template.
One of my cards on the OVP shows an KPI header with the following values:

  • KPI Value: Average Delivery Delay (in days) for the past 90 days
  • Target Value: Average Delivery Delay (in days) for the past 365 days
  • Automatically calculated deviation of the values  in %

We recently upgraded our system from S/4 2020 SP3 to S/4 2023 SP1. This included updating SAPUI5 libraries from 1.84.6 to 1.120.0. After the upgrade, the deviation calculation shows a wrong value. Before it worked as expected.

                
<Annotation Term="UI.DataPoint" Qualifier="DataPointDurchschnittLieferverzug">
	<Record Type="UI.DataPointType">
		<PropertyValue Property="Title" String="{@i18n>datapoint_title_durchschnitt_lieferverzug}" />
		<PropertyValue Property="Description" String="{@i18n>datapoint_title_durchschnitt_lieferverzug}" />
		<PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="AvgDelivToCmtdDelivDelayInDays" />
		<PropertyValue Property="CriticalityCalculation">
			<Record Type="UI.CriticalityCalculationType">
				<PropertyValue Property="ImprovementDirection" EnumMember="UI.ImprovementDirectionType/Minimize" />
				<PropertyValue Property="ToleranceRangeHighValue" Decimal="3" />
				<PropertyValue Property="DeviationRangeHighValue" Decimal="5" />
			</Record>
		</PropertyValue>
		<PropertyValue Property="TrendCalculation">
			<Record Type="UI.TrendCalculationType">
				<PropertyValue Property="ReferenceValue" Path="AvgDlvToCmtdDlvDelayPast365Day" />
				<PropertyValue Property="UpDifference" Decimal="1" />
				<PropertyValue Property="StrongUpDifference" Decimal="2" />
				<PropertyValue Property="DownDifference" Decimal="-1" />
				<PropertyValue Property="StrongDownDifference" Decimal="-2" />
			</Record>
		</PropertyValue>
		<PropertyValue Property="ValueFormat">
			<Record Type="UI.NumberFormat">
				<PropertyValue Property="NumberOfFractionalDigits" Int="1" />
			</Record>
		</PropertyValue>
	</Record>
</Annotation>

How to fix this?

entry.title: Re: Insert text type and content in notes tab in Edit Freight Unit Fiori App
entry.content: You should not spend time on tagging the persons, you should focus on listing the appropriate "SAP Managed Tags". NB: I have no interest in "SAP Transportation Management".

entry.title: Come contattare il team di support di SAP Education – Learning Hub
entry.content:
** This blog was translated by my Italian speaking colleague Petra Sunjic

State cercando supporto dal Learning Hub o dal Training Team e non sapete da dove cominciare? Questo blog è per voi…

In questo articolo, condividerò i passaggi che dovete seguire per interagire con l'area di supporto; gli agenti sono disponibili 24 ore su 24, dal lunedì al venerdì, e la maggior parte delle richieste riceve risposta entro 24 ore.

Cliccate qui se desiderate leggere questo blog in inglese, tedesco o spagnolo.

Nella piattaforma di supporto troverete una sezione dedicata alle FAQ, che offre risposte rapide alle domande più frequenti.

  • Temi generali di supporto
  • Sistemi di pratica
  • Formazione per partner rivenditori
  • Consigli su apprendimento e certificazione
  • Prenotazione corsi e gestione dei pagamenti
  • Pannello di analisi

Come creare un caso

Se le informazioni che sta cercando non sono presenti nella piattaforma, può creare un caso per ricevere supporto per la sua richiesta specifica.

Basta accedere con il Suo Universal ID e creare un ticket.

Dal sito SAP Learning Hub:

  1. Cliccate sull'icona del punto interrogativo (?) situata in alto a destra della pagina
  2. Nella pagina del Centro di supporto, cliccate sul pulsante “Contattaci” e selezionate “Richiedi assistenza tecnica”.
  3. Scegliete l’opzione “Avete bisogno di più aiuto?”
  4. Effettuate il login con il vostro Universal ID e cliccate su “Crea caso”.

Dal sito SAP Learning:

  1. Scorrete fino in fondo alla pagina e cliccate su “Ottieni supporto”, che si trova nella sezione Supporto e Contatti.
    .
  2. Sarete reindirizzati alla pagina del Centro di supporto utenti, dove troverete le FAQ e guide dettagliate per l’auto-aiuto.
  3. Se non trovate l’aiuto necessario nelle FAQ, scorrete verso il basso e cliccate sull’icona “Avete bisogno di aiuto?” per accedere alla pagina successiva e aprire un ticket.
  4. Completate la procedura cliccando sul pulsante “Crea caso”.

Inoltre, potete utilizzare il link diretto per accedere al Centro di supporto utenti.

Per visualizzare gli aggiornamenti del vostro ticket, vi consigliamo di cliccare sul pulsante di aggiornamento prima di cercare il vostro caso.

In che modo il Learning Team può aiutarvi?

Il Learning Team può aiutarvi se desiderate ottenere informazioni su corsi, contenuti di certificazione e percorsi di apprendimento. Inoltre, può consigliarvi su quale apprendimento sia necessario per raggiungere i vostri obiettivi.

Altri servizi includono:

  • Domande di primo livello sulle certificazioni, inclusi consigli su come configurare correttamente il vostro UID e accedere alle credenziali.
  • Problemi relativi a informazioni, consegna e assistenza post-corso per le sessioni condotte da istruttori.
  • I problemi relativi alle prenotazioni e alla fatturazione sono generalmente gestiti da un altro team interno, ma il team di apprendimento può intervenire per approvare eventuali nuove prenotazioni gratuite e situazioni simili, in quanto spesso sono legati a reclami da parte dei clienti, ecc.
  • Lettere per il visto.
  • Fornire informazioni relative ai fornitori su richiesta.

Richedere supporto in una lingua diversa.

Se desiderate che il team di apprendimento vi assista nella lingua preferita, potete selezionare la lingua dopo aver inserito la descrizione, selezionando il paese in cui vi trovate e scegliendo tra: Tedesco, Inglese, Spagnolo, Francese e Giapponese.

Vi invitiamo anche a leggere il nostro blog per scoprire tutto sulle certificazioni SAP e sull’Universal ID - SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! 

Inoltre, questa KBA potrebbe esservi utile: 2404504 - Come ottenere aiuto con il SAP Learning Hub o il SAP Training Center.

Grazie per aver letto!

entry.title: Re: Chinese names in learning as alternative name
entry.content:
Hi Janine,
We had the same question for one of our customers.
Finally we decided to use the LMS user field Middle name (MI)
This was only done after analysing that the Middle Name wasn't filled by anyone.
We renamed the middle name in LMS to Local Name.
There is a token called 'User Middle Name' which you can use in the certification of Completion.
Optionally you could also amend the name format (First Name, Middle Name, Last Name) for The Chinese locale https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2670640 
Therefore you need to update the Label ID: format.LastName.FirstName.MI.

Regards, Bas

entry.title: Re: SAP instance reached state SHUTDOWN after having stat...
entry.content: Please see the updated files, thanks once again

entry.title: Re: SAP VIM Workflow completed with error and invoice document has created
entry.content: We have the same issue. ¿How do you solved it?

entry.title: IDOC LOIWCS AND QCERT AUTOMATION
entry.content:
Hello Specialists.

I have a problem with sending Outbound IDOCs, with the message type LOIWCS and QCERT. In the case of LOIWCS, I created a REPORT where I check the modification date, fill in a table, and submit it to the RCCLWCEN program. Is this a possibility?

Because I couldn't find a BADI to help me. For the QCERT case, I need to send an Inspection Plan, but what path do I need to follow to execute the sending of this type?

entry.title: Re: Localized Demo Databases Now Available for SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2305

Check SAP Note 3011710: Deploy Demo Databases in SAP Business One after download

Could you let me know it? I'm using the same email of this account to acces the link but it don't follow trough.

entry.title: Re: Enable/Disable custom action button in SAP Fiori List...
entry.content: Hi, I tried using 'applicablePath' in button's property with in manifest.json. The value to this 'applicablePath' is entity type property from entityset with boolean type.

entry.title: Re: RAP : To Show Child or not to Show Child, depending o...
entry.content: if that boolean value can be calculate easily based on cds data, you can avoid the abap class, just cast( your case statement as abap_boolean) as is***sectionhidden, something like that.))

entry.title: Improve Community 2024 feed to append "and X other people" to "mentioned you"
entry.content:
In my Notification feed, I receive messages like this:

In fact, the message targets a bunch of people, so it's not really a message targeting me specifically:

Is it possible to change the message "@mentioned you in" to "@mentioned you and 11 other people in"?

Thank you.

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Importing Scope from the Digital Discovery Assessment into SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
Hi Tomasz,

Just to add: The warning about the selection exceeding the recommended scope limit is also displayed in case that the number of selected scenarios is 100 or more. As mentioned, this is to ensure optimal performance. Hope that helps.

Best regards, 
Fabian

entry.title: Re: Improve Community 2024 feed to append "and X other pe...
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi - I already noticed that question.
I will add your feedback to our list of UI change requests to check what is possible here. And as always, get back to you with updates. (we are working on your previous request for label changes in the notification feed)

entry.title: How to influence Community 2024 developments?
entry.content:
Is there an official way to contact SAP Community to ask for some fixes or improvements of https://community.sap.com? Right now, I'm posting them at Questions about SAP Websites | SAP Community, hoping that SAP people are looking at it...

I couldn't find any "SAP Community" group at https://influence.sap.com.

EDIT: posting to Questions about SAP Websites | SAP Community seems the right path, as can be inferred from these two replies by @AnnePetteroe: Improve Community 2024 feed to append "and X other... - SAP Community and Meaningless "posted a topic/replied to topic", ins... - SAP Community.

entry.title: Re: Improve Community 2024 feed to append "and X other pe...
entry.content: Oh, nice, thank you 🙂

entry.title: Difficulty with "no overlapping" operations
entry.content:
I'm having difficulty with "no overlapping" operations.

Operations 0050 and 0060 are "no overlapping", but operation 0060 is starting before 0050.

- 0050: ends on 12/19/2024 at 2:03:00 PM
- 0060: starts on 12/18/2024 at 4:32:37 PM

Both have the "No overlapping" option checked and both Work Centers have the "Can be used by several operations" option unchecked.

 

Can someone help me?

entry.title: Re: unable to use targetElement within ui.facet when exte...
entry.content: show me how you extend the cds with new association, did you expose the association?

entry.content: Sorry @Loes, eventually it worked, but I can't remember what we did to solve it. However, the business partner was not part of the solution

entry.title: Re: Sap CCO SAP B1 Integration error (The path is not acc...
entry.content:
Hi Tharindu Wijesinghe,

Kindly update sender from 'Integration Framework' and re-check the integration.

Setup Path : Scenarios -> Setup ->Sap Customer CheckoutUI & SAP Customer Checkout.

For reference please find attached screenshots.

 

Please let me know if you still facing this issue.

Thanks & Regards,

Rohit Nandan Verma

entry.title: SAP invoice split if line items reached to -240
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

There is a requirement from business to split the billing if billing lines reached to 240.

here in our business scenario we are creating multiple sales orders having 1 line & later doing invoice (order related billing) by clubbing all the sale orders in one invoice (using VF04).

But here we want to split the line if lines reached to line-240.

I have tried the way to maintain the same in SD-BILLING- BILLING DOCUMENT-COUNTRY SPECIFIC FEATURES with copy control routine-006 & its working if the order has multiple lines.

but what I said , when we are clubbing multiple orders in 1 invoice then its not working. 

Please suggest if any one has faced this requirement.

entry.title: How to add a new item and rename it in Purchase orders?
entry.content:
How to add a new item and rename it in Purchase orders? How and when can a new product can be added in a Purchase order?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add a new item and rename it in Purchase orders?
entry.content:
If the Status of the PO is Finished, new items cannot be added. 

New item can be added only when the status of the PO is in - In preparation, Sent, Follow up document created. New item can be added as below :

  1. Open the Purchase order 
  2. Navigate to Items tab 
  3. Click on Add Row 
  4. Enter the product ID , the description will be auto filled based on the data maintained in the product master data of the product ID. Product description cannot be modified or entered manually at the time of creation of purchase order.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 PUBLIC CLOUD - WHAT IS IT ABOUT
entry.content:
Hi @RahulBhatia20 , 

I suggest you re-post this as a blog, instead of Q&A.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: unable to use targetElement within ui.facet when exte...
entry.content:
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'CEXTADDRESS'
@EndUserText.label: 'Extension view'

extend view C_DFS_PositionHeaderTP with c_ext_address
association [1..*] to C_ZADDRESSVIEWTP as _ViewAddressTP on $projction.dfspositionId = _ViewAddressTP.DfsObjid

{
@ui.hidden: true
DfsPositionId as poshead, // no need to this element, just to silence the compilator
_ViewAddressTP
}

entry.title: E-Learnings for Getting Started with SAP LeanIX are now available!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Signavio Community, 

SAP Signavio Value Map, along with the SAP LeanIX Academy team, is excited to announce our next e-Learning course, “Getting Started with SAP LeanIX”.  This E-Learning is on the SAP Enterprise Support Academy and consists of three courses. If you haven’t done so already, I would recommend starting with the first e-Learning session we released ( SAP LeanIX Onboarding) and then move forward with “Getting Started with SAP LeanIX” e-Learning.

What is the purpose of these sessions?

By utilizing these first step courses you will quickly get up to speed and running with SAP LeanIX.  Again, we recommend you complete the SAP LeanIX Onboarding e-learnings first to ensure you have defined and added the most important date to your workspace and gotten your stakeholders on board.

What are the session goals?

Course:  Roll-out and Adoption Toolkit

  • Discover what resources are available in this self-serve toolkit to create user engagement and build a lasting EA (Enterprise Architecture) community in your wider organization
  • Learn where to access and download templates for roadmaps, communications, and more
  • Learn where to access how-to videos to share with your colleagues that cover the most basic functions of SAP LeanIX

Course:  Diagrams

  • Learn the basics of SAP LeanIX diagram editors
  • Understand how to model enterprise architecture effectively with free draw and data flow diagrams

Course:  SAP LeanIX for Fact Sheet Owners

  • Learn to navigate SAP LeanIX with ease and use key features
  • Understand how to provide accurate data input to your organization
  • Gain insight on maintaining application data in your SAP LeanIX workspace

How to access?

NOTE:  You MUST be registered with the SAP Enterprise Support Academy in order to access the “direct links” to these courses below (and can do so right here if not currently registered).

Get started with SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value Maps here. (Check out our step-by-step instructional video  on how to join SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value maps.)

Direct links to the new E-Learning courses (once registered as stated above).

  • Course:  Roll-out and Adoption Toolkit
  • Course:  Diagrams
  • Course:  SAP LeanIX for Fact Sheet Owners

Do you need additional help?

Don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation" topic).

Regards,

Leean De Loria

entry.title: Re: Call a released oData service from a RAP BO behavior...
entry.content: Hi, unfortunately, this doesn't suit our requirement. Our requirement is to call an odata service from a custom action to retrieve data.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation – SMTP Mail Destination using Microsoft 365 email provider
entry.content: @KarthikeyanMurthyAny chance of a new blog covering the OAuth2ClientCredentials as that was released in October? The SAP docu isn't all that clear on which parts of the MS docu are relevant/required and a step-by-step guide like this one would really help us when coordinating with Entra/O365 teams unused to this setup.

entry.title: Re: C4C Mashups: How to return data from a mashup
entry.content: Is something like this possible in SAP Sales Cloud V2?
